### Concurrency Tradeoffs
Given the constraint of **one heavy model server at a time** within a memory-safe Docker harness:
1.  **Parallelism is Counter-Productive**: Running multiple benchmarks concurrently would cause OOM (Out of Memory) crashes or severe context-switching overhead, negating any throughput gains.
2.  **Resumability Strategy**: The system must serialize execution. Concurrency should be limited to **I/O operations** (logging, state persistence) while the model inference runs on the main thread or a dedicated single worker.
3.  **Watchdog Integration**: A single-threaded execution model simplifies watchdog timeout enforcement, ensuring the "heavy" process is the only one consuming resources.

### C# API Design

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public sealed class BenchmarkState
{
    public string Id { get; init; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string Repository { get; init; } = string.Empty;
    public string Model { get; init; } = string.Empty;
    public List<string> Constraints { get; init; } = new();
    public List<double> Results { get; init; } = new();
    public bool IsComplete { get; set; }
    public DateTime LastCheckpoint { get; set; }
}

public interface IBenchmarkRunner
{
    Task<BenchmarkState> RunAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct = default);
    Task<BenchmarkState> ResumeAsync(string stateId, CancellationToken ct = default);
    void Cancel(string stateId);
}

public class LocalModelBenchmarkRunner : IBenchmarkRunner, IDisposable
{
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _singleInstanceLock = new(1,
